content:
Hey Guys

Starting from the middle of January 2026 I'm completely unable to send replies in patch threads from my gmail account without hitting spam filter (Reason 5.7.1. Rejected). I even tried to configure google workspace with my own custom domain without any luck.

Before that I was trying to use yahoo, but it was cutting my emails without even informing me.

I wonder which email service can I use to be 100% sure that it will not filter my letters out? I assume at this point I'll have to pay for it.

Thanks in advance

Cheers
Igor Korotin
I am using yandex with custom domain since quite long time and never experienced any issue

If the issue is the high volume of the kernel mailing lists, then you may want to take a look at https://public-inbox.org/lei.html and https://korgalore.docs.kernel.org to reduce that.

And for submitting kernel patches, you could use the https://b4.docs.kernel.org web endpoint.

@Igor Korotin Since you are listed in MAINTAINERS you may also qualify for [1].

[1] https://korg.docs.kernel.org/linuxdev.html

Miguel Ojeda said:

If the issue is the high volume of the kernel mailing lists, then you may want to take a look at https://public-inbox.org/lei.html and https://korgalore.docs.kernel.org to reduce that.

And for submitting kernel patches, you could use the https://b4.docs.kernel.org web endpoint.

Not exactly the case for me, if I understand correctly. I wanted just to ack some Rust I2C related patches, but 3 days in a row I'm getting spam blocked by gmail SMTP.
